moves.startingMovesNote: You start with Anger is a Gift, Potential for Greatness, and 2 other moves of your choice.

backgrounds.label: Impetuous Youth
backgrounds.description:
Stonetop has always been home, but you chafe at the demands of mundane life and have always longed for more. Excitement! Danger!

When you make a move and come up short, you can give it your all and turn a 6- into a 7-9, a 7-9 into a 10+, and (if it matters) a 10-11 into a 12+. But if you do, pick 1 (the GM will fill in the details): you get hurt (2d4 damage and an actual injury); you cause collateral damage, endanger others, or otherwise escalate the situation; or something on your person is lost or breaks.

backgrounds.label: Driven
backgrounds.description:
You once led a simple life, but something happened. Something changed you, burdened you with terrible purpose. What was it? Choose 1: a loved one was killed or abducted; someone gave their life to save you; your idol sacrificed themselves to save many; you stumbled upon a dark mystery; or you must make amends for a terrible mistake.

You always have the option to Burn Brightly; you can spend 2 XP after you roll to add +1, even if you don't have enough XP to level.

backgrounds.label: Destined
backgrounds.description:
Fate has laid her hand upon you. Choose 3-4 items to describe your destiny.

At the start of a session, roll +Omens: on a 7+, lose all Omens and the GM will share a vision or portent that points toward your fate; also, on a 10+, ask the GM a follow-up question and get a clear, helpful answer; on a 6-, don't mark XP, hold +1 Omen, and tell us of your recent nightmares or a troubling vision.

Until your destiny is fulfilled, treat a 6- on Death's Door as a 7-9, and a 7-9 as a 10+.

origin.region: Lygos or some other point south

options.label: A good dog
options.description: a loyal follower; HP 6; Damage d4 (hand, harmless); Instinct: to guard & protect; Cost: love and care.

options.label: Heap of expectations
options.description: the hopes and dreams of all who believe in you. Of little practical use, but it's there.

options.label: Husbandry tools
options.description: brushes, muzzles, collars, feed, ◇ whips, ◇ bridles, etc. Gain advantage to Persuade domestic beasts (livestock, dogs, etc.).

options.label: Personal token
options.description: something that marks your place as a would-be hero.

options.text: A family heirloom (reroll one failed roll per session)
A lucky charm (mark it; clear when you Make Camp; while marked, +1 forward)
A trophy from a past victory

options.label: Smithy
options.description: (or access to it): iron goods, ingots, thick gloves, ◇ tongs, ◇ bellows, an anvil, etc.

options.label: Stoneworker's tools
options.description: chisels, drills, ◇ prybars, ◇ spikes, ◇ block & tackles, wheelbarrow, etc.

options.label: Tannery
options.description: (or access to it): hides, pelts, alum, salt, ◇ frames, ◇ knives; leather goods, straps, armor, etc.

stonetop.statsNote: Assign these scores to your stats: +1, +0, +0, +0, +0, -1

list.description: Fire, burning, the smell of charred flesh.
That they won't take you seriously.
That you really aren't cut out for this.
The death of your family or loved ones.

list.title: What Makes You Burn with Righteous Anger?

list.description: The despoiling of beauty and innocence.
Violence to children, animals, the innocent.

list.title: When Did Your Fear or Anger Last Cause You Trouble?

list.text: When did your fear or anger last cause you trouble?

options.description: To refuse to back down, give up, give in.
To question yourself, your actions, your worth.
To prove yourself, to yourself and others.
To assume the best, and that things are simple.
To put the needs/wants of others above your own.

system.description: Most people hope for a quiet life. They spend their days a-worrying: about a leaky roof, a sick child, their crops. But you aren't like most people—you're on a different path. A path to adventure! There's greatness in you. Let's hope you live long enough for everyone else to see it.
